title: A measure for genuine tripartite entanglement
authors: Shengjun Wu, Kaichen Zhong, Jeffery Wu
abstract: We introduce a single real-valued functional $I(\vec{n}_1,\vec{n}_2)$, built from four three-qubit correlation expectation values, that turns the Greenberger--Horne--Zeilinger (GHZ) algebraic paradox into a \emph{quantitative} witness of genuine tripartite entanglement. We prove that for every three-qubit state $ρ$ and every pair of measurement directions $|I(\vec{n}_1,\vec{n}_2;ρ)|\le 2$, with the bound saturated if and only if the two measurement bases are mutually unbiased and $ρ$ is locally unitarily equivalent to the GHZ state. We obtain a closed-form expression for $I(\hat{x},\hat{y})$ on the five-parameter Acín canonical family of three-qubit pure states. For the W state we show that $I(\hat{x},\hat{y})=0$ and that $\max_{\vec{n}_1,\vec{n}_2}| I_{W}|=35/27\approx 1.296$, strictly below the GHZ value. The induced quantity ranges in $[0,1]$, equals one only on the GHZ class, and is therefore a device-independent indicator of GHZ-type genuine tripartite correlation. We also outline a generalisation of $I$ to three-qudit systems built from the Heisenberg--Weyl operators, recovering the standard qubit construction when $d=2$.
summary: The paper introduces a new mathematical functional that quantifies how much a three-qubit state exhibits the GHZ algebraic paradox. This allows for a clear, quantitative distinction between GHZ and W-type entanglement. The method is also shown to be extensible to higher-dimensional qudit systems.
main_problem: Developing a quantitative, device-independent measure to identify and distinguish genuine tripartite entanglement, specifically focusing on GHZ-type correlations.
main_result: The authors introduce a functional $I$ that acts as a witness for GHZ-type entanglement, reaching a maximum of 2 for GHZ states and strictly less for W states, with a derived measure $E_{GHZ}$ that ranges from 0 to 1.
method: The study uses algebraic construction of a real-valued functional from Pauli correlation expectation values, analytical optimization over measurement directions, and parameterization via the Acin canonical family.
model_or_system: Three-qubit quantum systems, with proposed generalizations to three-qudit systems using Heisenberg-Weyl operators and spin-S systems.
key_observables: Three-qubit correlation expectation values (Pauli spin observables) and the functional $I(\vec{n}_1, \vec{n}_2)$.
important_parameters: Measurement directions $\vec{n}_1, \vec{n}_2$; the Acin canonical family parameters; the dimension $d$ for qudit generalization.
main_assumptions: The measure is analyzed primarily within the context of local unitary equivalence and the GHZ/W entanglement classes; monotonicity under the full SLOCC hierarchy is not yet established.
figures_summary: Fig 1(a) shows the monotonic increase of the functional relative to state parameters; Fig 1(b) compares the supremum of the functional across different entanglement classes (GHZ, W, biseparable, and product states).
paper_structure: Introduction of the functional, proof of the universal upper bound, analysis of the Acin canonical family, comparison with the W state, generalization to qudits, and discussion of experimental feasibility.
why_interesting: It provides a new, quantitative, and device-independent tool for characterizing multipartite entanglement, which is crucial for verifying GHZ-type states in photonic or trapped-ion quantum computing platforms.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) The functional |I(ˆx, ˆy)| evaluated on the Ac´ın canonical family (14) depends only on the product µ = λ0λ4, with the closed form |I| = 8µ3 + 2µ [Eq. (17)]. The maximum value 2 is attained at µ = 1/2, the GHZ state. The W state has µ = 0 in its canonical form, giving I(ˆx, ˆy) = 0. (b) Supremum over orthogonal measurement directions, sup⃗n1⊥⃗n2 |I(⃗n1,⃗n2)|, for four representative three-qubit states. The GHZ state saturates the algebraic bound 2; the W state achieves 35/27 ≈1.296 (independently verified analytically, App. C); the biseparable state |0⟩A ⊗|Φ+⟩BC saturates 1 = 1
